analogy: Think of "Conservation of Plants and Animals" through the chapter's own opening images: In the previous chapter, you have learnt how animals reproduce. It is only after ‘growing up’ to a certain age that human beings and many other animals can reproduce. Why can humans reproduce only after a certain age? In this chapter, you will learn about... The easy idea is that the visible surface is only the outside cover. The chapter keeps asking how we can know what is inside when no one can directly reach the deep interior. So the learner must read clues: rocks, mines, drilling, volcanic material, heat, gravity, magnetism and earthquake waves. Like solving a hidden-layer puzzle, each clue tells something about the crust, mantle, core and volcanic activity without inventing anything beyond the textbook.
full_notes:
Conservation of Plants and Animals is decoded here only from the NCERT chapter text. The chapter begins by asking how the nature of the earth should be imagined and why the interior cannot be known in the same direct way as the surface. It connects the hidden interior with visible results on the surface, especially landscape development, earthquakes, tsunamis and volcanic activity.

The text explains that in the previous chapter, you have learnt how animals reproduce.

The text explains that it is only after ‘growing up’ to a certain age that human beings and many other animals can reproduce.

The text explains that why can humans reproduce only after a certain age?

The text explains that in this chapter, you will learn about changes that take place in the human body after which a person becomes capable of reproduction.

The text explains that in Chapter 6, you have learnt about human reproductive organs.

The text explains that here, we shall discuss the role that hormones play in bringing about changes that make a child grow into an adult.

The text explains that 7.1 Adolescence and Puberty Boojho was celebrating his 12th birthday.

The text explains that after his friends left, Boojho and Paheli began chatting with their parents.

The text explains that she remarked that many of Boojho’s school friends, whom she met after a year, had suddenly shot up in height.

The text explains that some of them were looking very funny with a hairy line above their lips.

The text explains that her mother explained that the boys had grown up.

The text explains that but upon crossing the age of 10 or 11, there is a sudden spurt in growth which becomes noticeable.

The text explains that the changes taking place in the body are part of growing up.

The text explains that they indicate that you are I wonder how long this period marked by changes in the body will last! no longer a child but are on the way to becoming an adult.

The text explains that it is a strange period of life when you are neither a child nor an adult.

The text explains that i wonder whether this period between childhood and adulthood had a special name!

The text explains that endocrine glands release hormones directly into the bloodstream.

The text explains that voice of boys becomes hoarse as voice box enlarges during adolescence. ‹ Children gain height during adolescence. ‹ The onset of puberty and maturity of reproductive parts are controlled by hormones. ‹ Hormones are secretions of endocrine glands which pour them directly into the blood stream. ‹ Pituitary gland secretes hormones which include growth hormone and hormones that make other glands such as the testes, ovaries, thyroids and adrenals, secrete hormones.

The text explains that write a two- minute speech explaining why early marriage is not good for the couple.

The text explains that collect newspaper cuttings and information in magazines about HIV/ AIDS.

The text explains that write a one page article of 15 to 20 sentences on HIV/AIDS.

The text explains that in our country, according to 2011 census, there are 940 adolescent females for every 1000 males.

The text explains that (a) the concerns of the community regarding this low ratio.

The text explains that remember that the chance of having a boy or a girl is equal.

The text explains that (b) what amniocentesis is and how useful this technique is.

The text explains that why is its use for identification of sex of the unborn child banned in India?

The text explains that put your ideas together and write a short note on the importance of knowing facts about reproduction.

The text explains that for more information visit : l www.teenshealth.org/teen/sexual_health/ l www.adolescenthealth.com 2024-25

Source: NCERT Class 8 Science, Chapter 7: Conservation of Plants and Animals

revision_bullets: In the previous chapter, you have learnt how animals reproduce. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)
It is only after ‘growing up’ to a certain age that human beings and many other animals can reproduce. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)
In this chapter, you will learn about changes that take place in the human body after which a person becomes capable of reproduction. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)
In Chapter 6, you have learnt about human reproductive organs. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)
Here, we shall discuss the role that hormones play in bringing about changes that make a child grow into an adult. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)
7.1 Adolescence and Puberty Boojho was celebrating his 12th birthday. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)
After his friends left, Boojho and Paheli began chatting with their parents. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)
She remarked that many of Boojho’s school friends, whom she met after a year, had suddenly shot up in height. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)
Some of them were looking very funny with a hairy line above their lips. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)
But upon crossing the age of 10 or 11, there is a sudden spurt in growth which becomes noticeable. (NCERT Class 8 Science Ch7 pg 1)

prelims_traps: Do not treat 7.1 Adolescence and Puberty and REACHING THE AGE OF ADOLESCENCE REACHING THE AGE OF ADOLESCENCE as identical unless the source text links them directly.
Do not add outside examples to Conservation of Plants and Animals; use only the examples and processes named in the chapter.
Do not confuse a visible surface result with the internal process or source evidence described by the text.
mains_angles: Explain Conservation of Plants and Animals through the source chain: evidence, process and visible result.
Use 7.1 Adolescence and Puberty and REACHING THE AGE OF ADOLESCENCE REACHING THE AGE OF ADOLESCENCE to show how the chapter connects facts with mechanisms.
Frame an answer around causes, internal working, surface expression and source limitations from the NCERT chapter.

In girls, adolescence may begin a year or two earlier than in boys.

The human body undergoes several changes during adolescence.
